latest_revision_note: 2026-06-20 revision after E group roster/player_state completion and public lineup/odds check: Germany vs Cote d'Ivoire remains 2-1 with Germany probability nudged to 60%; Ecuador vs Curacao remains 2-0 representative score but Ecuador win probability rises to 70% and 3-0 becomes a strong alternate.
scope: E group round 2 match predictions with betting-risk gates

risk_gate.red_team_policy: no main-pick language as steady banker when hold/revise gates remain

market_snapshot.type: public odds snippet, not full same-book live snapshot
market_snapshot.market_note: Model is slightly cooler on Germany because of post-7-1 public-bias risk, rotation risk, and Cote d'Ivoire transition threat.

china_sports_lottery_notes: Germany win is the first direction but not a steady banker.
If handicap is Germany -1, do not rely only on handicap win.
invalidate_if: Germany rotates multiple core attackers or Kimmich
Cote d'Ivoire counterattack starters are all fit and market pushes Germany further
same-book odds move sharply without team-news confirmation

market_snapshot.type: public odds snippet, not full same-book live snapshot
market_snapshot.market_note: Model is materially cooler on Ecuador because Ecuador finishing risk remains and Curacao may be over-penalized after 1-7.

china_sports_lottery_notes: Ecuador win is the first direction but low price may reduce value.
If handicap is Ecuador -1, handicap win and draw both need evaluation; if -2, avoid chasing.
Main total-goal band is 2 with 3 as cover.
invalidate_if: Curacao changes to a deeper back five and odds over-punish them
Ecuador market price shortens without lineup support

aggregate_notes: Germany and Ecuador are the first-direction favorites, but neither should be treated as a steady betting banker before lineups and full odds.
Group E round 2 requires guarding against post-round-1 overreaction: Germany may be overpriced after 7-1; Curacao may be over-punished after 1-7.
Draw baseline is kept higher than a pure market read because recent G/H process correction requires stronger protection against favorite conversion overconfidence.
